pattern: Detecting that two racing pointers collide only proves a loop exists; resetting one pointer to the head and walking both one step at a time turns that meeting point into the exact entrance of the loop.
hints: A visited-set finds the first repeated node in linear extra space; the harder version asks for constant space, so think about what a slow/fast pointer pair can tell you beyond just 'yes there is a loop'.

Once the slow and fast pointers collide somewhere inside the loop, the distance from the list head to the loop's entrance equals the distance from the collision point back around to that same entrance.
Reset one pointer to the head, keep the other at the collision point, and advance both a single step at a time — the node where they next coincide is the cycle's start; return that node directly.

edge_inputs.description: cycle back to the second node
two nodes looping to the head
tail loops onto itself only, entrance is the last node
duplicate values everywhere — only node identity, never value, can locate the entrance
cycle entrance well past the midpoint of a longer list

stress.description: random list length with a randomly placed cycle roughly 60% of the time, or none at all
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, max(1, min(size, 500)))
    values = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    pos = rng.randint(0, n - 1) if n and rng.random() < 0.6 else None
    return [{"values": values, "pos": pos}]
